zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Allen於1688年出生在美國維吉尼亞州查爾斯 希迪 庫迪查爾斯 希迪時，她的父親Valentine Richard Allen58歲，母親Mary Jane Page58歲。她於1733年5月3日與William Gerard Tucker在美國維吉尼亞州威斯特摩蘭威斯特摩蘭結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1722年5月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州威斯特摩蘭享年34歲。

zh-hans.Charles City, Charles, Virginia, United: 美国弗吉尼亚州查爾斯 希迪 庫迪查爾斯 希迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Allen于1688年出生在美国弗吉尼亚州查爾斯 希迪 庫迪查爾斯 希迪时，她的父亲Valentine Richard Allen58岁，母亲Mary Jane Page58岁。她于1733年5月3日与William Gerard Tucker在美国弗吉尼亚州威斯特摩蘭威斯特摩蘭结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1722年5月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州威斯特摩蘭享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Allen was born in 1688, in Charles City, Charles City County, Virginia, United States, her father, Valentine Richard Allen, was 58 and her mother, Mary Jane Page, was 58. She married William Gerard Tucker on 3 May 1733, in Westmoreland, Westmoreland, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 3 May 1722, in Westmoreland, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 34.

Lei sposato William Gerard Tucker il 3 maggio 1733, nel Westmoreland, Westmoreland, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Allenが1688年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州チャーレス シトイ コウントイチャーレス シトイで生まれた時，父Valentine Richard Allenは58歳，母Mary Jane Pageは58歳であった。1733年5月3日にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州ウエストモーエランドウエストモーエランドでWilliam Gerard Tuckerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1722年5月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ウエストモーエランドで34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Allen(이)가 1688년 미국 버지니아 주 찰스 시티 카운티 찰스 시티에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Valentine Richard Allen(은)는 58세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Jane Page(은)는 58세였다. 그녀는 1733년 5월 3일 미국 버지니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 웨스트모렐랜드에서 William Gerard Tucker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1722년 5월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Allen nasceu em 1688, em Charles City, Charles City County, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Valentine Richard Allen, tinha 58 anos e sua mãe, Mary Jane Page, tinha 58 anos. Ela casou-se com William Gerard Tucker em 3 de maio de 1733, em Westmoreland, Westmoreland, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 3 de maio de 1722, em Westmoreland, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Allen родилась в 1688 г. в местности чарльс сити, чарльс сити коунти, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Valentine Richard Allen было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Jane Page, 58. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Gerard Tucker 3 мая 1733 г. в местности вестмореланд, вестмореланд, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 3 мая 1722 г. в местности вестмореланд, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Allen nació en 1688, en Charles City, Charles City County, Virginia, Estados Unidos, su papá, Valentine Richard Allen, tenía 58 años y su mamá, Mary Jane Page, tenía 58 años. Se casó con William Gerard Tucker el 3 de mayo de 1733, en Westmoreland, Westmoreland, Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 3 de mayo de 1722, en Westmoreland, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 34 años.
